sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Bayer 04 Leverkusen have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with a heavy loss to PSG (2-7) but strong domestic wins like 3-4 against Mainz. SC Freiburg, on the other hand, remain unbeaten in their last 9 matches, securing draws and narrow wins, including a 2-0 victory over FC Utrecht. Leverkusen’s attacking prowess contrasts with Freiburg’s resilience and defensive consistency.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Leverkusen have dominated with a 5-1 win at home and a 2-2 draw away at Freiburg. Historically, Leverkusen are unbeaten in their last 5 matches against Freiburg, with both teams often scoring, as seen in 8 consecutive head-to-head games.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Leverkusen rank 5th with 14 points, boasting a strong attack (16 goals, 3rd in the league) and solid possession play (57%, 3rd). Freiburg, sitting 11th with 9 points, have a balanced but less potent attack (11 goals), though their late-game scoring (highest in 76-90 minutes) could pose a threat.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Leverkusen’s Patrik Schick, with 3 goals in recent matches and a historical edge over Freiburg (6 goals in 5 games), will be crucial. Freiburg will rely on their defensive organization and late surges, though no standout player has emerged in their recent form.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: With Sofascore predicting a 54% chance of a home win, Leverkusen are favorites at the BayArena, given their attacking depth and historical dominance. Freiburg’s unbeaten streak and ability to score late could make it a close contest, likely resulting in both teams scoring.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Bayer 04 Leverkusen leads SC Freiburg 1-0 at the BayArena. The home side took the lead through a well-taken goal by Ernest Poku in the 22nd minute. Leverkusen has dominated possession and shown intent in the final third, but Freiburg has not been without their chances, displaying resilience and creating dangerous moments of their own.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: Leverkusen has controlled the game with a significant share of possession and a decent number of entries into the final third. Their attacking play has been spearheaded by players like Ernest Poku, who not only scored but also looked lively throughout the half. The home side created a notable opportunity but missed a big chance, which could have extended their lead. Defensively, they've been relatively solid with multiple clearances, though their duel-winning stats suggest room for improvement against Freiburg's physical approach.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: SC Freiburg has shown determination despite trailing at half-time. They've had their fair share of entries into the penalty area and even outperformed Leverkusen in terms of shots on target. The away side missed a big chance to equalize, which could come back to haunt them. Their ability to win duels, especially in the air, has kept them competitive, and they've shown promise in winning possession in dangerous areas. However, they need to convert their opportunities to get back into this game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Freiburg showing early intent, pushing forward with consistent attacking spells in the opening minutes. However, Leverkusen gradually took control with a significant shift in momentum around the middle of the half, leading to their goal. After the goal, the home side maintained pressure for a while before Freiburg fought back with renewed vigor toward the latter stages of the half. The closing minutes saw the away team mounting several attacking waves, indicating their desire to level the score before the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Leverkusen, Ernest Poku has been the standout performer with his goal and overall threat in attack, making him a likely candidate to influence the second half further. Aleix García also impressed with his creative contribution in setting up plays. On Freiburg's side, Derry Scherhant has shown potential to unlock the Leverkusen defense with key passes, while Junior Adamu looked dangerous with his attempts on goal. Both teams have players capable of turning the game in their favor.
